superduck CLI test page

Open this file in a Chrome tab tracked by the SuperDuck extension, grab the tabId via ./superduck tabs, and run the commands below against it. Each widget shows whether it received the expected event.

1. Clicks (left / right / double / triple)

Triple-click selects this whole sentence to verify the browser saw 3 clicks.

Status: waiting

2. Hover

Hover me
you hovered!

Status: waiting

3. Type & Key

Use cmd+a / ctrl+a via superduck key to select all.

Last event: —

4. Drag (left_click_drag)

DRAG
DROP HERE

Status: waiting

5. Scroll (viewport)

Call superduck scroll <x> <y> --direction down --amount 5 — then check the bottom box.

you reached the bottom ✅ (scroll position: 0)

6. scroll_to (via ref)

Use a tool that produces ref ids (e.g. read_page) to scroll to this element: 📍 scroll target

7. Zoom (region screenshot)

Capture this red pixel grid with superduck zoom 40 40 220 220 --output /tmp/.

8. form_input target (use read_page to get refs)

Last submit shown in event log.

8b. File upload (use upload tool with --ref)

Last upload: —

9. console / network playground

Use superduck console --pattern superduck and superduck network --url-pattern /api/.

Event log
